reasoning: Based on the Wikipedia table for "List of infectious diseases," the status for a vaccine for Gonorrhea was historically blank (No), and it remained so up to the knowledge cutoff of October 2025. While there is research underway for a Gonorrhea-specific vaccine (e.g., GSK's vaccine in phase 1/2 trials) and an off-label use of the Meningitis B vaccine (Bexsero) exists in parts of the UK, there are no specific, approved vaccines solely against Gonorrhea close to licensure. A new vaccine entirely completing Phase 3 and getting approved in the 3 months following the cutoff date is practically impossible. The only path to the prediction resolving to "Yes" lies in a Wikipedia editor deciding to fill in the blank on that specific list by adding the MenB vaccine as a form of cross-protection, but since this hasn't been done over the past several months since it made the news, the probability of it suddenly happening in the specified very short forecasted windows (1 week, 1 month, 3 months) remains very low. Consequently, I give very low, slightly increasing probabilities.
